issue_title: Outbox: move to drafts not working all the time

reasoning_trace.finding: Confirmed repository context: Thunderbird for Android includes K-9 Mail as a white-label app built from the same codebase.
Confirmed repository conventions and that legacy code still exists; this task is static fault localization, not an architectural change.
The prompt-mentioned local attachment directory/file was not present in the checked-out repository, so no issue log contents were available locally.
Search located Outbox/Drafts handling primarily in legacy UI and legacy core controller code, especially MessagingController.moveToDraftsFolder().
The newer message-list feature has OutboxFolderEvent only for sending pending messages; it does not define the reported Move to Drafts action.
Effects include navigation and discarded-draft messages but no direct Move to Drafts effect for Outbox messages.

Found concrete UI entry points in MessageViewFragment, MessageListFragment, and LegacyMessageListFragment; all call MessagingControllerWrapper.moveToDraftsFolder() or MessagingController.moveToDraftsFolder().

The message-view overflow menu shows R.id.move_to_drafts when viewing Outbox and dispatches R.id.move_to_drafts to onMoveToDrafts(), which calls messagingController.moveToDraftsFolder(account, folderId, messages).

The selected-message action mode shows Move to Drafts for Outbox when the account has a drafts folder and calls messagingController.moveToDraftsFolder(accountId, currentFolder.databaseId, selectedMessages).
The legacy list fragment has the same selected-message path as MessageListFragment, confirming both reproduction variants share the same controller path.
Wrapper method moveToDraftsFolder(id, folderId, messages) resolves the account DTO and delegates directly to MessagingController.moveToDraftsFolder(account, folderId, messages).
moveToDraftsFolder() posts background work; moveToDraftsFolderInBackground() loads each Outbox message, calls saveDraft(), and only calls message.destroy() if saveDraft() returns a non-null draft ID.

The conversion from Outbox to Drafts depends on saveDraft() returning a message ID. If it returns null, the original Outbox message is left untouched, producing the visible 'nothing happens' behavior.

saveDraft() chooses saveAndUploadDraft() whenever messagingController.supportsUpload(account) is true. saveAndUploadDraft() saves locally, queues a PendingAppend, then immediately calls messagingController.processPendingCommands(account).

For upload-capable accounts, Move to Drafts is made dependent on immediate remote upload of the new draft. Any MessagingException from processPendingCommands() is caught by saveDraft(), logged, and converted to null.
The local draft save data is created from the loaded message and is not itself tied to Outbox-specific UI state; the intermittent nature is more consistent with the later remote processing step.
LocalMessage.destroy() completely removes the original Outbox row. It is only reached when draftSavedSuccessfully is true, so failure before this call leaves the Outbox message visible.

loadMessage() can throw when a message is missing, but that would be a stale-reference/concurrency edge. The shared 'sometimes nothing happens after sending error' behavior is better explained by saveDraft() returning null after a remote draft upload failure.

reasoning_trace.target: UI menu visibility and selection handlers
reasoning_trace.finding: Both message view and message list expose and dispatch the Move to Drafts action correctly; the fault is not the menu item dispatch.

destroy() is downstream and only runs after a successful draft save. If it were the fault, the draft would still be saved and an exception would be expected, not an intermittent no-op tied to sending errors.

reasoning_trace.target: DraftOperations.saveAndUploadDraft() should not require immediate remote draft upload for Outbox-to-Drafts conversion
reasoning_trace.finding: The most likely fault is the call to processPendingCommands(account) inside saveAndUploadDraft(), because it turns an otherwise completed local draft save into a null result when upload fails, preventing removal from Outbox.

fault_candidates.method: saveAndUploadDraft(LegacyAccountDto account, Message message, Long folderId, Long existingDraftId, String subject)
fault_candidates.reasoning: Move to Drafts for Outbox messages calls saveDraft(); for upload-capable accounts this method saves the draft locally, queues a remote append, then immediately processes pending commands. If that remote processing fails intermittently, saveDraft() catches the MessagingException and returns null. MessagingController.moveToDraftsFolderInBackground() then skips message.destroy(), so the Outbox message remains and the user observes that nothing happened.

fault_candidates.statement: Long draftMessageId = saveDraft(account, message, null, message.getSubject());
fault_candidates.reasoning: This is the direct call whose null result causes the operation to become a no-op. However, the deeper cause is that DraftOperations.saveAndUploadDraft() makes the save result depend on immediate remote upload success.

final_answer.method: saveAndUploadDraft(LegacyAccountDto account, Message message, Long folderId, Long existingDraftId, String subject)
final_answer.causal_chain: User chooses Move to Drafts in Outbox -> MessageViewFragment or MessageListFragment calls MessagingController.moveToDraftsFolder() -> moveToDraftsFolderInBackground() loads the Outbox message and calls saveDraft() -> DraftOperations.saveDraft() routes upload-capable accounts to saveAndUploadDraft() -> saveAndUploadDraft() saves a local draft and queues PendingAppend, but then immediately calls processPendingCommands(account) -> an intermittent remote/upload MessagingException during this call makes saveDraft() return null -> moveToDraftsFolderInBackground() sees draftSavedSuccessfully == false and does not call message.destroy() -> the original message remains in Outbox, so the user sees no move.
final_answer.fix_hint: Decouple Outbox-to-Drafts local conversion from immediate remote draft upload: return the local draft message ID after queuing the append, and let pending command processing retry asynchronously instead of making the UI action fail.
